quoteFirst: "The attack was renewed by the enemy
quoteTime: about seven o'clock in the morning
quoteLast: , and was bravely resisted by the limited number of troops I could bring into action until eleven o'clock, when orders were given for the army to establish itself on the new line. This it did in good order. The position I abandoned was one that I had held at a disadvantage; and I kept the troops on it as long as I did, only for the purpose of enabling me to hear of the approach of the force under Gen. Sedgwick.
title: The Campaign of Chancellorsville
author: Dodge, Theodore Ayrault, 1842-1909

quoteLast: , with my entire staff, I rode along a portion of our front, and when in the open field before Appler's regiment, the enemy's pickets opened a brisk fire upon my party, killing my orderly, Thomas D. Holliday, of Company H, Second Illinois Cavalry. The fire came from the bushes which line a small stream that rises in the field in front of Appler's camp, and flows to the north along my whole front.
title: Memoirs of General W. T. Sherman, Volume I., Part 2
author: Sherman, William T. (William Tecumseh), 1820-1891

quoteFirst: The sleep of the three adventurers would have, perhaps, been indefinitely prolonged if an unexpected noise had not awakened them

title: The Moon-Voyage

quoteFirst: of the 7th to move by the shortest and best route to Rocky Mount, thence to Martinsville, or Henry Court House, to be there by 9 o’clock on the morning of the 8th, and there await the balance of the brigade, which was to move by a different route. The distance was 75 miles. The regiment moved at 4 a. m., and by a forced night march reached Henry Court House
title: A brief history of the Tenth Michigan Cavalry
author: Trowbridge, L. S. (Luther Stephen), 1836-1912

quoteLast: She is tall, slim built, and a little stooped; brown hair, blue eyes, long, thin, pale face. Dressed in a full suit of black.

quoteFirst: A most decided change seemed to have taken place; still the barometer remained as low as on the previous evening. A slight breeze from south-east changed to north, and at
quoteLast: the rain began to fall. Clouds of nimbus closed on the woody horizon, and we had a day of rain. In the evening the barometer had fallen still lower, and it was probable that the rain might continue through the night. Range of thermometer from 74° to 72°.
